executable.title: GradientAD_nkrchak
executable.description: Performs gradient anisotropic diffusion on the input image
executable.acknowledgements: Hans J. Johnson provided starter code and XML file for SimpleThreshold. This builds from SimpleThreshold.xml.

parameters.label: Filter Parameters
parameters.description: Parameters for the gradient anisotropic diffusion filter

double.name: conductance
double.label: Conductance
double.description: Conductance for gradient anisotropic diffusion filter

constraints.minimum: Although conductance can be set to 0, it is very rare for the user to need that capability, so 0.1 is the minimum conductance for this application<--> 0.1
constraints.step: Fine-grain step size for choosing the conductance<--> 0.1
constraints.maximum: 5.0 is a very high conductance value. This is mainly used for images with heavy noise but it can make smaller structures disappear due to smoothing<--> 5.0

double.name: timeStep
double.label: Time Step
double.description: Time step for gradient anisotropic diffusion filter
double.constraints:
Stable time step is determine by 0.5/2^N equation where N is the number of dimensions<--> For a 2-d image this maximum is already 0.125 so there isn't a need to have the maximum beyond 0.13<--> Step size is 0.01 for ease of use, and the minimum is 0.01 to match the step size magnitude <-->
0.01
0.01
0.13

integer.name: iterations
integer.label: Iterations
integer.description: Number of iterations for gradient anisotropic diffusion filter

constraints.minimum: The true minimum number of iterations, if the iterations is set to 0 the algorithm would do "nothing"<--> 1
constraints.step: Allows the user to tune the number of iterations in a fine-grain matter. This is the smallest integer step-size<--> 1
constraints.maximum: 20 iterations is a high number of iterations for curvature anisotropic diffusion, but could be needed in some cases. Anything beyond 20 is seen as detrimental to the algorithm<--> 20
